title: immunodeficiency, but not for 23andMe users
detail: For 23andMe users, this is the normal form, you can safely ignore it. However when oriented to current versions of dbSNP, this is a very rare genotype and linked to immunodeficiency This rare homozygous genotype has been reported to be the basis of primary immunodeficiency-20 in several publications. However, due to the orientation issues (and confusion) connected to A/T ambiguous flips and the assignment of this SNP to the reverse strand in dbSNP in at least some versions of dbSNP, be aware that this may be a false assignment if you see it in your Promethease report and it should be independently checked/verified.
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.

title: associated with elevated C-reactive protein levels and hypertension
summary: associated with elevated C-reactive protein levels and hypertension
detail: CRP gene codes for C-reactive protein, a marker of inflammation. C-reactive protein (CRP) gene polymorphisms, CRP levels and risk of incident essential hypertension: findings from an observational cohort of Han Chinese. (2012). [http://www.nature.com/hr/journal/v35/n10/full/hr201289a.html] Plasma CRP levels were independently associated with the development of hypertension. The minor alleles of rs1130864 (P<0.001) and rs3093059 (P<0.001) were significantly associated with elevated CRP levels. Plasma CRP levels were substantially associated with common genetic variants in the CRP gene and can predict the development of hypertension.
